text:
Problem Overview

This problem offers students a chance to scale ratios using the context of a slime recipe. Students will have the opportunity to practice strategies discussed in earlier problems. Students will be pushed to think about comparison statements that do not always have whole-number quantities.

Note: The context of slime could be changed to another recipe that your students find interesting and ask similar questions that are in the Initial Challenge and What If...? situation.

Arc of Learning: Exploration, Analysis

Standards: See your state alignment chart.

NWDYK?

What strategies did you use to solve problems that involve ratios and unit rates?

Emerging Mathematical Ideas

  • Continue building expertise in working with unit rate to solve ratio problems of greater complexity. Work to find needed equivalent ratios unit a variety of strategies, including
    • unit rates;
    • ratio tables;
    • multiplying/dividing both quantities in a ratio (scaling) to find larger or smaller values as needed; and
    • algebraic expressions to generate equivalent ratios.
